Metadata.Coram: Sundaresh Menon CJ, Andrew Phang Boon Leong JA, Steven Chong JA
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: Court of Appeal of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Parakou Investment Holdings Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: In November 2008, Parakou Investment Holdings Pte Ltd purchased 10 vessels and two uncompleted hulls, collectively known as the OPL Vessels, from Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd
Between 12 and 24 November 2008, Parakou Investment Holdings Pte Ltd received S$9,812,543 in debt repayments from Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the sale of the OPL Vessels to a related company was a transaction at an undervalue, and whether the related company is liable for dishonest assistance regarding debt repayments
Party_Details.Rule: A transaction is at an undervalue if the consideration received is significantly less than the value of the asset, and a third party is liable for dishonest assistance if it knowingly participates in a breach of fiduciary duty
Party_Details.Application: The sale of the OPL Vessels was executed at a fair market price after factoring in a standard fleet discount, and the subsequent debt repayments were received legitimately.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The Court of Appeal allowed the appeal in part. It found that the sale of the OPL Vessels was not at an undervalue due to the applicability of a fleet discount, but affirmed PIH's liability for dishonest assistance and knowing receipt regarding the PIH Repayments.

Metadata.Coram: Sundaresh Menon CJ, Andrew Phang Boon Leong JA, Steven Chong JA
Metadata.Tribunal_Court: Court of Appeal of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd (in liquidation)

Facts.Text: Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd was incorporated in 1995 with Mr Liu Cheng Chan and Mdm Chik Sau Kam as its shareholders and directors
In November 2008, Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd sold the OPL Vessels to Parakou Investment Holdings Pte Ltd
Parakou Shipping Pte Ltd entered creditors' voluntary liquidation in April 2011

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the sale of the OPL Vessels to a related company was a transaction at an undervalue, and whether the related company is liable for dishonest assistance regarding debt repayments
Party_Details.Rule: A transaction is at an undervalue if the consideration received is significantly less than the value of the asset, and a third party is liable for dishonest assistance if it knowingly participates in a breach of fiduciary duty
Party_Details.Application: The sale of the OPL Vessels was rushed and executed at an undervalue to strip the company of its assets, and the related company dishonestly assisted in the directors' breaches of fiduciary duties.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The Court of Appeal allowed the appeal in part. It found that the sale of the OPL Vessels was not at an undervalue due to the applicability of a fleet discount, but affirmed PIH's liability for dishonest assistance and knowing receipt regarding the PIH Repayments.
